name: Radio Online
description: Automated online radio streaming plugin using Liquidsoap. Stream audio to Icecast servers with weekly playlist scheduling via Liquidsoap Telnet control.
overview: Automated online radio streaming via Liquidsoap to Icecast servers with weekly playlist scheduling. Assign Jellyfin playlists to specific day/time slots. Requires a Liquidsoap Docker container running alongside Jellyfin.

versions.changelog:
CRITICAL FIX: ResetState() was called AFTER loading playlist tracks, wiping _playlistTracks to empty array — the for loop ran 0 times and no tracks were ever sent to Liquidsoap
FIX: Replaced ResetState() with _nextTrackToSend=0 to only reset the send index without destroying the loaded playlist
ROOT CAUSE: In v0.0.0.37, _playlistTracks was set at line 411, then ResetState() at line 431 cleared it — queue.append was never called

FIX: Plugin stuck in Inactivo state after failed queue attempts — _currentScheduleKey was set before verifying tracks sent, causing the plugin to never retry loading the playlist
FIX: _currentScheduleKey now only set on successful queue, cleared on failure so next cycle retries automatically
IMPROVE: Error message now includes 'will retry on next cycle' for clarity

ARCHITECTURE: Push metadata — Liquidsoap now TELLS the plugin what's playing via HTTP POST, eliminating all sync issues
NEW: TrackChangeController — HTTP endpoint at /RadioOnline/TrackChange receives track notifications from Liquidsoap
REMOVE: All sync code eliminated — _currentTrackIndex, VerifyTrackSyncAsync, TryResumeCurrentPlaylistAsync, OnReconnected, queue.remaining
SIMPLIFY: RadioStreamingHostedService now only manages buffer (keep queue.length >= 3) — no timing, no guessing
SIMPLIFY: LiquidsoapClient — removed OnReconnected event, HadPreviousConnection, GetQueueRemainingAsync
IMPROVE: Buffer refill based on queue.length instead of estimated timing — 100% reliable
IMPROVE: Resume on restart still works via queue.current_track + queue.length query
IMPORTANT: You MUST add on_track HTTP callback to radio.liq — see release notes
IMPORTANT: Remove queue.remaining command from radio.liq (no longer needed)

FIX: Plugin restart no longer resets playback to track 0 - on fresh start, queries what Liquidsoap is actually playing and resumes from that position (no audio interruption)
FIX: _currentTrackIndex desync eliminated on restart - Liquidsoap keeps playing uninterrupted while the plugin repositions its tracking to match
NEW: TryResumeCurrentPlaylistAsync - queries queue.current_track on startup, matches against playlist, and resumes tracking from the correct position
NEW: queue.remaining Telnet command for accurate track timing on resume (calculates _currentTrackStartedAt from remaining time)
NEW: GetQueueRemainingAsync in LiquidsoapClient
IMPROVE: ResetTrackingState moved after resume check - state is only reset if resume fails
IMPROVE: queue.length used on resume to estimate _lastQueuedTrackIndex, avoiding duplicate track queuing
NOTE: Resume only works for non-shuffle playlists (shuffle order changes each restart)
NOTE: You MUST add queue.remaining command to radio.liq - see release notes

FIX: Track desync after TCP reconnection - plugin now performs full bidirectional resync when Liquidsoap reconnects, correcting _currentTrackIndex to match actual playback
FIX: Backward drift detection - plugin now detects and warns when Jellyfin shows a song ahead of what Liquidsoap is actually playing (index converges naturally)
FIX: Reconnect resync bypasses 60-second throttle - runs immediately on reconnect event instead of waiting for next periodic check
NEW: OnReconnected event in LiquidsoapClient fires when a new TCP connection replaces a dropped one (not on first connect)
NEW: HadPreviousConnection property exposes whether a prior connection existed for meaningful reconnect detection
IMPROVE: VerifyTrackSyncAsync now uses exact match (Ordinal) instead of case-insensitive match for track path comparison

FIX: Duplicate tracks and out-of-order playback - removed queue depth resync that incorrectly reset _lastQueuedTrackIndex backward during crossfade transitions
FIX: queue.length now uses non-deprecated 'string()' function in radio.liq
IMPROVE: Queue depth monitoring is now diagnostic-only (warning log) without modifying playback state
NOTE: Update radio.liq with the corrected queue.length command from release assets

FIX: Playback interruptions eliminated - proactive TCP reconnection every 2 minutes prevents stale half-open connections that caused silent command failures
FIX: Queue depth verification - plugin now queries Liquidsoap's actual queue length before trusting local index, automatically resyncing when mismatch detected
FIX: Read timeout reduced from 10s to 3s - stale commands no longer block the semaphore for extended periods
IMPROVE: Buffer increased from 1 to 2 tracks ahead (3 initial tracks instead of 2) - tolerates multiple consecutive send failures before audio gap
NEW: queue.length Telnet command for real-time queue monitoring (requires radio.liq update)
NOTE: You MUST update your radio.liq to add the queue.length command - see release assets

NEW: Multi-day schedule support - each program can now repeat on multiple days via checkboxes (L M X J V S D)
NEW: Pause toggle per program - temporarily disable any schedule entry without deleting it
NEW: Unique entry IDs for reliable schedule change detection across day boundaries
IMPROVE: Schedule display shows compact day badges (active days highlighted in blue)
IMPROVE: Backward compatible - old single-day entries still work without migration
FIX: Overlap priority rule preserved - latest start time always wins

FIX: Track index only advances when the next track is confirmed queued in Liquidsoap - prevents gaps and offair interruptions
FIX: Failed track sends are retried on the next cycle instead of being skipped
FIX: Double playback reporting eliminated - each track is now reported exactly once using _lastReportedTrackIndex
FIX: Buffer maintenance improved - AdvanceTrackAsync wrapped in try/catch to prevent cycle crashes on telnet errors
FIX: Connection timeout resilience - if advance fails, the system recovers gracefully on the next 1-second cycle

FIX: Track advancement now works regardless of playback reporting setting - tracks are always sent one-by-one with buffer
FIX: Playback reporting is optional and does not affect track delivery
FIX: One-by-one mode sends all playlist tracks correctly (initial 2 + buffer as each track ends)

NEW: Playback reporting to Jellyfin statistics - tracks are sent one-by-one with buffer for accurate PlayCount and LastPlayedDate updates, enabling smart playlists (most played, least played, recently played)
NEW: Configurable option 'Report playback to Jellyfin statistics' in plugin settings
NEW: Track duration detection from Jellyfin metadata for precise advance timing
FIX: No longer requires queue.current_track telnet command (works with all Liquidsoap versions)
FIX: Playback reporting filters out tracks without duration metadata
IMPROVE: Tracks sent in small batches (2 initial + 1 buffer) instead of all at once - less telnet load, faster playlist transitions

NEW: Playback reporting to Jellyfin statistics - each track played on the radio now increments PlayCount and updates LastPlayedDate, enabling smart playlists (most played, least played, recently played, etc.)
NEW: Configurable option 'Report playback to Jellyfin statistics' in plugin settings
NEW: Liquidsoap queue.current_track telnet command for track monitoring
FIX: DLL now correctly reports AssemblyVersion 0.0.0.26 (was loading 0.0.0.24 due to stale Directory.Build.props)
FIX: Playback reporting filters out off-air audio and external radio streams
IMPROVE: Path-to-item mapping preserved through shuffle for accurate reporting

NEW: Playback reporting to Jellyfin statistics
NEW: Configurable option in plugin settings
NEW: Liquidsoap queue.current_track telnet command
FIX: DLL AssemblyVersion fix

FIX: Schedule now stops at exact programmed time - inverted clear+skip order (clear queue FIRST, then skip current track) so skip falls through to off-air instead of starting next queued track
FIX: Second playlist now properly takes priority when schedules overlap - clears queue and immediately loads next schedule
FIX: Times no longer drift/accumulate between schedules
FIX: Exception handler now clears Liquidsoap queue to prevent orphaned tracks from playing after errors
IMPROVE: Near-end polling checks every 1 second (instead of 5) when less than 30 seconds remain in schedule slot for precise timing

FIX: Dark theme dropdown menus in scheduling section (option backgrounds match Jellyfin dark theme)
FIX: Minute selector now shows all 60 minutes (00-59) instead of 5-minute increments
FIX: Schedule now ends at exact programmed time - skips current track and triggers crossfade instead of waiting for song to finish
FIX: Playlist switching at exact time with crossfade transition (skip + clear + load)
IMPROVE: Remaining time check prevents songs from playing past schedule end

FIX: Telnet Broken pipe - SendCommandAsync now retries once after reconnecting on IOException
FIX: Schedule not followed - now tracks full schedule key (day+time+playlist) instead of just playlist ID
FIX: Queue clear now retries up to 3 times on failure to ensure schedule transitions work reliably
FIX: Status endpoint shows correct telnet state after reconnect
